english: 1. (resp. śańs (shangs)) the nose, B.; in col. language sna-mtsúl (sna mtshul), v. below; snai rús-pa (snai rus pa) bridge of the nose, snai °čag-krúm (snai 'chag krum) cartilage of the nose; skad sná-nas °dón-pa (skad sna nas 'don pa) to utter (nasal) whining tones Mil.; sná-nas °khríd-pa (sna nas 'khrid pa) to lead or turn by the nose; sna °pyí-ba (sna 'phyi ba) to blow one's nose. — 2. trunk, proboscis, pág-pai (phag pai) Glr.; gláń-sna (glang sna) v. glań (glang). — 3. a mountain projecting from some other mountain in a lateral direction, a spur Glr.; it might also be used for cape, promontory. — 4. end, thig-sna (thig sna) the end of a string Glr., rál-pai sna (ral pai sna) the end of a lock of hair Glr.; hem, edge, border, gós-kyi sna (gos kyi sna) the border of a garment Cs.; esp. the nearer end, fore-part, °od čén-po źig-gi sná-la ('od chen po zhig gi sna la) foremost of a bright ray of light (that was approaching) Mil.; sna °drén-pa (sna 'dren pa) to lead, to head (a body of men) cf. mjug-ma (mjug ma); dmág-sna °drén-pa (dmag sna 'dren pa) to take the command of an army Pth.; more indefinitely, like °drén-pa ('dren pa): to draw along, to lead, to guide, esp. with lam (lam), to direct the way or course of a person, (having the person always in the genit. case); °gro drúg-gi lam-sna °dren ('gro drug gi lam sna 'dren) as a guide he leads all beings Mil.; *čú-na ḍem-pa* (chu na dem pa) C. to conduct water (by a water-course); to bring upon, to cause, v. below, compounds; rnág-sna °drén-pa (rnag sna 'dren pa) to cause suppuration Med.; lám-sna °dzín-pa (lam sna 'dzin pa) to have taken a certain road Mil. — In some cases it is difficult to account for the signification, so: sna-čén-po (sna chen po) Cs. a deputy; commissioner; sna-lén byéd-pa (sna len byed pa) c. genit. to shelter, harbour, lodge, take in, Pth., C.; sna (b)stád-pa (sna (b)stad pa) Lex., bdág-gi sna-stád kyód-la re (bdag gi sna stad khyod la re) Cs. I place my full confidence in you; *ná-do tóg-nẹ* (na do thog ne) C., (*nár-do gyáb-te* (nar do rgyab te) W.) *°gyél-ba* ('gyel ba) either: to fall by striking with the fore-part of one's foot against a stone, or by striking one's foot against a stone lying before one. — 5. sort, kind, species, mostly with tsógs (tshogs) (-pa (-pa)), W. with *so-só* (so so), diverse, various, all sorts of, spos sna-tsógs-kyis °débs-pa (spos sna tshogs kyis 'debs pa) Dzl. to strew all sorts of spices over . . . .; rnám-pa sna-tsógs (rnam pa sna tshogs) frq.; less frq. sna-mań (sna mang) Lex., sna dpag-tu-méd-pa (sna dpag tu med pa) Glr., sna-tsád (sna tshad) Glr. of every sort; rín-po-če sna-bdun (rin po che sna bdun) seven kinds of jewels; dár-sna lńa (dar sna lnga) five sorts of silk; also sna (sna) alone is added to substantives, inst. of sna-tsógs (sna tshogs), or = rnams (rnams): śiń-snai dúd-pa (shing snai dud pa) smoke from different sorts of wood Glr.; °brú-sna smíń-pa ('bru sna smin pa) the ripening of corn Glr.; sna-gčíg (sna gcig) a single one Mil.; čós-sna (chos sna) Tar. 166, 4 prob. is not so much a kind, as a part of doctrine, Schf. — 6. mí-sna (mi sna), bló-sna (blo sna) v. mi (mi) and blo (blo). Comp. sná-skad (sna skad), *ná-kad ton* (na kad ton) W., he speaks through his nose. — sna-kúń (sna khung) nostril. — sna-khrág (sna khrag), sna-khrág °dzág-pa (sna khrag 'dzag pa) a bleeding from the nose, sna-khrág gčód-pa (sna khrag gcod pa) to stop it, čad (chad), it ceases, it is stanched. — sna-khríd (sna khrid) guide, leader; the leader of a choir. — sná-ga (sna ga) col. = sna (sna) 3. — sna-góń (sna gong) trunk, proboscis Sch. — sna-sgáń (sna sgang) bridge of the nose Cs. — sna-sgrá (sna sgra) the noise made through the nostrils Cs., snuffling. — sna-čú (sna chu) a running nose, sna-čú °dzag (sna chu 'dzag) mucus is dropping from the nose Lt. — sna-čén (sna chen) Thgr. a demon (?). — sna-mčú (sna mchu) an elephant's trunk Pth. — sna-tág (sna thag) 1. a rope passed through the nose of a beast to lead it by. 2. proboscis, sna-tág (sna thag) or sna-mčú sríń-ba (sna mchu sring ba) to stretch it forward Pth. — sna-drí (sna dri) prob. = snabs (snabs) Med. — sna-gdóń (sna gdong) bridge of the nose Sch. — sna-°dág (sna 'dag) (spelling?) W. snuff. — sna-°drén (sna 'dren) leader, commander; sdug-bsńál-gyi sna-°drén (sdug bsngal gyi sna 'dren) one that causes misfortune, author of it. — sna-nád (sna nad) disease of the nose. — *na-čī* (na chi) C., *na-pí* (na pi) W., pocket-handkerchief. — sna-bábs (sna babs) the glanders Sch. — sná-bo (sna bo) 1. leader, commander, chief. 2. a guide, gom gsum tsam-laań sná-bo dgos (gom gsum tsam la'ang sna bo dgos) about every third step one wants a guide Mil. — sna-búg (sna bug) S.g., sna-sbúgs (sna sbugs) Cs., nostril. — sna-sbyóń (sna sbyong), sna-smán (sna sman) snuff Med. — sná-ma (sna ma) Lex. w.e., Cs. = sna (sna) 4. — sna-rtsá (sna rtsa) root of the nose Cs. — sna-rtsé (sna rtse) tip of the nose. — sna-tsógs (sna tshogs) v. sna (sna) 5. — *nam-tsúl* (nam tshul) W., *nam-súl* (nam sul) Bal. = sna (sna) 1 and 2. — sna-°dzúr (sna 'dzur) an aquiline or crooked nose Cs. — sna-léb (sna leb) a flat nose Cs. — sna-śá (sna sha) the flesh of the nose; the nose Cs.; sna-śá sbyíń-pa (sna sha sbying pa) to suffer
